page: Vultr voc-c-16c-32gb-300s-amd is an instance in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor built on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz with 16 physical cores and a 1.0 thread-per-core allocation. The system is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 300 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2935.67, placing it in the top 25% tier. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 46446.97 and a PassMark CPU score of 33071.36, both falling within the middle 50% average. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show an average read-only small block speed of 2128.51 GB/sec and a large block write speed of 71.14 GB/sec. Database operations and web serving capabilities are also average, with Redis SET operations reaching 30.51 million operations per second and static web serving throughput measured at 16.16 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a high core count and fast local NVMe storage relative to its resource tier, though the CPU allocation is shared, which may introduce performance variability under sustained multi-tenant load. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions this server as a compute-focused option rather than a memory-heavy system. It is suitable for workloads such as web serving, multi-threaded decompression tasks, moderate database operations, and general-purpose compute jobs that benefit from high single-core performance and fast local storage.
description: Vultr voc-c-16c-32gb-300s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, a 300 GB NVMe SSD, and one public IPv4 address. Operating at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation, the server delivers strong single-core performance, scoring 2935.67 in stress-ng tests, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance provides a compute-oriented balance suitable for web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and moderate database workloads. The shared CPU model offers a cost-effective entry point for high-core-count deployments, though users should consider potential performance variability under sustained multi-tenant demands.

tagline: A compute-focused virtual server featuring sixteen AMD EPYC cores, fast local NVMe storage, and strong single-core performance for parallel workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
300 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
